ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 11713004 represents water as a substance; used as closest parent since no specific mineral water product code is available.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large 1-litre bottle of mineral water, as typically served at a restaurant or purchased from a food delivery platform.

nutrient_source.title: Bottled Water, Mineral Water — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 14555)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for mineral water (bottled) used. Values reflect the composition of natural mineral water, which contains no macronutrients. Mineral content (sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium) reflects typical mineral water composition per USDA data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Water and Zero-Carbohydrate Beverages — University of Sydney GI Database Convention
gi_source.source_match_notes: Mineral water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and therefore has no measurable glycemic index. By convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is set to 'low'. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. This convention is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database treatment of non-glycemic beverages.

hypertension.guidance: Mineral water contains approximately 2 mg sodium per 100 ml, which is negligible and poses no meaningful sodium load for individuals managing hypertension; adequate hydration with mineral water is appropriate and encouraged.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Mineral water contains no carbohydrates, sugars, or calories and has no effect on blood glucose levels, making it the ideal hydration choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: No fat, cholesterol, or sodium of concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Mineral water is free of fat, cholesterol, and added sodium, and its naturally occurring calcium and magnesium content may provide modest cardiovascular benefit; it is a recommended hydration source for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mineral water (large bottle, 1 litre) is commercially bottled natural mineral water sourced from underground springs or aquifers, containing naturally occurring dissolved minerals such as calcium, magnesium, and trace amounts of sodium and potassium. It undergoes minimal processing — typically only filtration and bottling — with no added sugars, flavours, preservatives, or carbonation in the still variety. The water is clear, odourless, and tasteless beyond a subtle mineral character that varies by source. It contains zero calories, zero macronutrients (protein, fat, carbohydrate), and zero sugar, making it the most clinically neutral beverage available. It has no glycemic impact whatsoever. For individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease, mineral water is universally recommended as the primary hydration source. In Singapore, large bottles of mineral water (1 litre) are commonly served at Indian restaurants, hawker centres, and food delivery platforms as an accompaniment to meals. Popular brands available in Singapore include Evian, Perrier (still), and local brands. The mineral content — particularly calcium (approximately 33 mg per 100 ml in typical mineral waters) and magnesium (approximately 6 mg per 100 ml) — may contribute modestly to daily mineral intake.

How many calories in mineral water?

confidence_reason: Mineral water is a well-characterised food item with consistent nutritional composition (zero macronutrients) confirmed by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. The nutrient profile is universally agreed upon across all approved sources. No estimation or analogy was required for macronutrients. GI sentinel value of 1 is applied by convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as mineral water contains no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no protein by nature. fat_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no fat by nature. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no carbohydrates by nature. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. All vitamin fields are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — mineral water contains no vitamins. iron_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, phosphorus_mg are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for mineral water. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero. trans_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g are naturally zero. water_g is 100.0 as the product is entirely water. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. The food_id GD003656 originates from field survey data (Baba Eating House - South Bridge Road, tagged under Indian cuisine). The cuisine tag 'Indian' reflects the restaurant context rather than the food's intrinsic origin; mineral water is a universal beverage. The large bottle (L) designation corresponds to a 1-litre serving size standard in Singapore restaurant contexts. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record; all nutrient fields are either confirmed zero or have reported values from USDA FoodData Central.
